message:
fix(core): prevent shutdown deadlock, update API call sites, expand signal handling

Three targeted production fixes:

- IPC shutdown: convert shutdown_tx.send(()).await to try_send() to
  prevent deadlock when the receiver has already dropped during the
  shutdown sequence. The bounded channel send could block indefinitely
  if the receiver side was torn down first.

- Storage + robot response API alignment: update get_recent_segments()
  call to get_segments() (removed the unused include_archived boolean
  parameter) and rename RobotResponse::ok() to RobotResponse::success()
  in the anomaly-detection response paths, matching the upstream API
  renames.

- Runtime compat signal handling: add SignalKind::interrupt() (SIGINT)
  and SignalKind::hangup() (SIGHUP) to the asupersync signal backend,
  expanding beyond the existing terminate() (SIGTERM) for more complete
  graceful shutdown handling including Ctrl+C and terminal hangup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
